ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general iced tea food product class; no specific entry for canned sweetened lemon tea. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers sweetened tea-based beverages. No specific code for canned ice lemon tea; closest defensible category used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for canned sweetened lemon tea beverage; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one 300ml can, equivalent to approximately 300g, providing 105 kcal and 26.7g of sugar.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Beverages, tea, ready-to-drink, lemon-flavored, sweetened (FDC ID 174869; SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Fuze Tea Ice Lemon Tea is a commercially produced sweetened canned lemon tea. Nutrient values (energy 35 kcal/100g, carbohydrate 8.9g/100g, sugar 8.9g/100g, sodium 10mg/100g, vitamin C 6mg/100g) are consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry FDC ID 174869 'Beverages, tea, ready-to-drink, lemon-flavored, sweetened' and related branded food entries for RTD sweetened lemon tea. Protein and fat are confirmed zero by product formulation — this is a sugar-water-tea-lemon beverage with no protein or fat ingredients. Values are within the expected range for this product category per USDA approved-source data. Input data originated from a GrabFood delivery platform listing (a forbidden primary source); nutrient values were retained only where independently corroborated by USDA FoodData Central approved-source range.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal, American Diabetes Association)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Fuze Tea Ice Lemon Tea. GI estimated at 68 (medium) based on analogous sugar-sweetened beverage entries in Atkinson et al. (2008) 'International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values', Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283 — a peer-reviewed journal source. Sugar-sweetened soft drinks and RTD teas with comparable sugar content (~8–9g/100g) are reported with GI values in the range of 59–72 in this reference. GI of 68 represents the midpoint of this range and is the most defensible analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (68 × 26.7) / 100 = 18.2. Serving = 300g can; available carbohydrate = 26.7g (8.9g/100g × 300g). GL of 18.2 is classified as high per serving (GL ≥ 20 is high; 18.2 approaches high threshold).

hypertension.key_factor: High added sugar content (8.9g/100g; 26.7g per 300ml can) in a sugar-sweetened beverage with no fibre, triggering insulin-mediated sodium retention and independently elevating blood pressure risk
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this beverage; the 26.7g sugar load per can exceeds the AHA daily added sugar limit for women and drives insulin-mediated sodium retention that raises blood pressure independently of the beverage's low sodium content (10mg/100g).

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 8.9g sugar/100g (26.7g per can), zero dietary fibre, and medium-to-high glycaemic index (~68), causing rapid postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid this beverage entirely; the 26.7g sugar delivered in liquid form with no fibre buffering produces a rapid and significant glycaemic spike, and regular SSB consumption is independently associated with worsening insulin resistance and HbA1c.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High added sugar intake from SSBs is associated with elevated triglycerides, reduced HDL cholesterol, and increased cardiovascular event risk; this beverage contains 26.7g sugar per can with zero saturated fat or sodium concern
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid this beverage; habitual SSB consumption at this sugar level is linked to dyslipidaemia and increased CVD risk per AHA 2018 dietary guidelines, even in the absence of significant sodium or saturated fat.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar at 8.9g/100g promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine production, overriding the minor anti-inflammatory benefit of black tea polyphenols and vitamin C
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should replace this beverage with unsweetened black tea with fresh lemon to obtain genuine anti-inflammatory benefit without the sugar-driven inflammatory burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fuze Tea Ice Lemon Tea (Canned, 300ml) is a commercially manufactured, ready-to-drink sweetened lemon tea beverage sold in a 300ml aluminium can. It is produced by The Coca-Cola Company under the Fuze Tea brand and is widely available in Singapore through fast food outlets, convenience stores, and food delivery platforms. The beverage is made from water, sugar, black tea extract, lemon juice or lemon flavouring, citric acid, and added vitamin C (ascorbic acid). It is served chilled and has a light amber colour with a sweet, mildly tangy lemon-tea flavour and a thin, watery texture typical of canned RTD teas. Per 100g, it provides 35 kcal, 8.9g carbohydrate (all sugar), 0g protein, 0g fat, 10mg sodium, and 6mg vitamin C. A single 300ml can delivers approximately 105 kcal and 26.7g of sugar — equivalent to over 6.5 teaspoons. Clinically, this is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) and is unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its high liquid sugar content, medium-to-high glycaemic index (~68), and absence of dietary fibre. The vitamin C content (6mg/100g) provides minor antioxidant value but is insufficient to offset the metabolic risks of the sugar load. The beverage is halal-certified, vegan, vegetarian, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is commonly paired with Western fast food meals, fried chicken, and wings in the Singapore food service context.
common_user_queries: Is Fuze Tea Ice Lemon Tea healthy?
How much sugar is in Fuze Tea lemon tea?
Fuze Tea lemon tea nutrition facts

Fuze Tea vs fresh lemon tea health comparison
Is Fuze Tea suitable for weight loss?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values (energy 35 kcal/100g, carbohydrate 8.9g/100g, sugar 8.9g/100g, sodium 10mg/100g, vitamin C 6mg/100g) are consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry FDC ID 174869 for ready-to-drink sweetened lemon tea beverages and are plausible for a commercially manufactured product of this type. Protein and fat are confirmed zero by product formulation. GI is an analogous estimate from a peer-reviewed journal source (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care) rather than a direct product measurement, which limits confidence to medium overall.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero — this is a sugar-water-tea-lemon beverage containing no protein ingredients by formulation. fat_g is confirmed zero — no fat-containing ingredients are present in this product by formulation. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero by product formulation. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero — no fibre-containing ingredients are present. potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this specific commercial canned lemon tea product are not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174869) or Singapore HPB Foodo database at the level of individual micronutrients beyond vitamin C as of 2026. GI of 68 is an analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. (2008) Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal based on comparable sugar-sweetened beverages; no direct GI entry exists for Fuze Tea Ice Lemon Tea. GL per serving = (68 × 26.7) / 100 = 18.2; serving = 300g can, available carbohydrate = 26.7g. Input data source was GrabFood (delivery platform, a forbidden primary source); nutrient values were retained only where independently corroborated by USDA FoodData Central approved-source range (FDC ID 174869). The original input listed 'foodo.sg' URL under HPB Singapore but the URL does not resolve to a specific HPB nutrient entry for this product; USDA FoodData Central used as primary approved source.
